other_observers: Brandon Percival, Duane Nelson, Stan Oswald; First found, I think, at this site in Nov (?) by Brandon Percival
specific_location: On west end of tires at South Marina, Pueblo Res.
description: A very large gull, the bird was on the tires with about 1000 other gulls and dwarfed all of them, including, even, the Herring Gulls. It's head was white with a very small amount of vague brown streaks. The eyes were small and yellowish. The bill was very large (thick and long) and yellow and sported a red gonydeal spot.  The neck, underparts, tail, and rump were white. The legs were pale pink. The mantle and the upper side to the wings were blackish, contrasting just a bit with the black wingtips. The scapular and tertial crescents were medium-sized. The p10 mirror was conbined with the white tip to that feather to create a very long white tip. There was a sizable p9 mirror and vague whitish tongues on pp5-7.Behaviors: Lying and standing on tires; flyingCall: None heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
